title: amphiblemma cuneatum
intro: Amphiblemma cuneatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Tetrameris, commonly found in tropical Africa. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including treating fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases, as well as reducing inflammation and fighting off parasites. Herbal preparations of Amphiblemma cuneatum can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the roots to make a decoction, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
